paragraph: The single-family market in Leonia is competitive but not frenzied. With 22 homes sold over seven months at a median price of $867,500, you're seeing about half the homes close above asking and a sale-to-list ratio barely above 1.0. Median days on market sit at 23, which means priced homes are moving, but sellers aren't in a rush and buyers have some room to negotiate if they find the right property. The price range is wide, from $585,000 to $2.2 million, so the market isn't one story. Condos and townhouses are thinner on the ground at ten sales over the same period with a median of $509,500. They're taking longer to sell, with a median of 44.5 days on market and only 10 percent closing above asking. The sale-to-list ratio dips to 0.99, which tells you buyers may find leverage in this category if they're willing to wait for the right unit. If you're comparing tiers, the deciding factor isn't market heat. It's whether you want the space and grounds of a single-family home or the lower price point and lower-maintenance lifestyle of a condo.
